title: Id newtype with non-empty invariant enforced at deserialization time is correct and must be preserved
description: Id is a newtype over String with a custom Deserialize impl that rejects empty strings immediately at parse time. Two construction paths exist: Id::new (fallible, for user-controlled input) and Id::from_trusted (panics in debug on empty, for server-assigned IDs and test fixtures). This is correct because JMAP IDs are opaque server-assigned strings that the spec defines as non-empty; allowing an empty Id to exist would propagate a malformed state into every field that holds one (chat_id, sender_id, etc.). A future contributor might suggest replacing Id with String to reduce boilerplate, or unifying the two constructors into one that silently skips validation. The first loses the compile-time distinction between validated and unvalidated IDs; the second allows the malformed state to propagate. The same design applies to UTCDate. The from_trusted escape hatch is explicitly documented as only acceptable for server responses and test fixtures.
